solution_text: An operating system is system software that manages hardware and software resources and provides services for computer programs.
Kernel is the core part of an operating system that manages system resources like CPU, memory, and devices.
System calls are the interface between user programs and the operating system to request services.
This answer depends on the concept. Generally, it explains the definition and working of the topic in operating systems.
